# Kaola-Workflow Roadmap

This file mirrors active unfinished work. GitHub issues are the source of truth when available.

## Active Work

| Issue | Title | Status | Workflow Project | Next Step |
|-------|-------|--------|------------------|-----------|
| #673 | fix(scripts): locateSection heading match uses startsWith vs classifier anchored ^##\s+H\s*$ — post-#670 residual | open | — | Surfaced by the #670 adversarial-verifier (R1). #670 closed the fence-INDENT divergence between schema.locateSection and the hash-covering classifier (both now anchor fences at ^\s{0,3}). The adversary then found a sibling divergence in the same parity family but on HEADING MATCHING: locateSection (adaptive-schema.js:1149 and :1165) selects a section via ln.startsWith('## ' + heading) / ln.startsWith('## '), a loose prefix match, while the classifier (classifier.js sectionBodyState / headRe) uses an anchored ^##\s+<heading>\s*$. Forward: looking for '## Node Ledger', locateSection matches a decoy '## Node Ledger Extra' the classifier does not (adversary-confirmed live with zero fences). Reverse: '##  Node Ledger' (two spaces) / '##\tNode Ledger' (tab) are matched by the classifier's \s+ but missed by locateSection's single-space prefix; a heading on line 0 is missed by locateSection's i>0 guard. Same plan_hash wedge class as #670, different channel. Fix (S-M): make locateSection's heading match byte-parity with the classifier's anchored ^##\s+<heading>\s*$ (exact heading, \s+ after ##, trailing-whitespace-only tail; reconcile the line-0 guard), apply byte-identically to all 4 adaptive-schema copies (byte-anchor group, NOT sync:editions), add a suffix/multi-space/tab/line-0 heading-parity regression. Do NOT touch the classifier (read-only oracle). Cross-edition four-chain. Note: the CRLF residual R2 is NOT a defect (both parsers are identically CRLF-blind; #670 removed a pre-fix trim CRLF divergence). Full body on GitHub #673. |
| #677 | fix(scripts): worktree-cleanup existsSync stat-failure still fails open + stale-sweep unprobeable-keep untested (post-#672) | open | — | Surfaced by the #672 review (R1) and adversary (A1, A2), live while verifying #672's worktreeDirtyState fail-closed fix. Two residuals in the same claim.js legacy/stale worktree-cleanup surface, OUTSIDE #672's porcelain-probe family. A2 (defect): worktreeDirtyState (claim.js:~480) hoists fs.existsSync OUT of the porcelain try to split 'missing' from 'unprobeable', but existsSync returns false for an EXISTING path when the parent dir is unreadable (chmod 000), so a present worktree routes to 'missing' → destructive consumers prune its registry entry + delete its merged-branch ref (content-safe — dir survives — but a stat-failure fail-open in the same family). Adversary-confirmed live via a chmod-000 parent. Fix (S): fail-closed the stat path too — lstatSync in try/catch, classify EACCES/ENOTDIR-on-parent as unprobeable not missing; regression on the parent-unreadable path asserting worktree+branch kept. A1 (test coverage): cmdStaleWorktreeCleanup's 'unprobeable' keep has no shipped regression (only cmdLegacyWorktreeCleanup tested); removing the guard on a scratch copy keeps the suite green while an ENOBUFS/unprobeable stale sweep deletes worktree+branch (behavior verified correct by #672 review + adversary, but untested). Add a mirrored cmdStaleWorktreeCleanup unprobeable-keep regression across --execute/--force/--archive/--export. Cross-edition (claim.js COMMON + divergent forge ports) → four-chain. Full body on GitHub #677. |
| #678 | fix(scripts): open-ready group baseline strands on group-form aborts (post-#674 symmetric gap) + pre-journal hard-crash window | open | — | Surfaced by the #674 adversary (R1) + code-review (R1/R2), both execution-confirmed. Two residuals in the same open-ready lane-group baseline lifecycle, adjacent to #674's member-baseline-drop fix. R1 (symmetric gap, low): #674 drops each recorded MEMBER baseline (barrier-base-<member> file+ref+freshness) on every post-baseline group-form abort, but the GROUP baseline (barrier-base-lg-<...> file + refs/kaola-workflow/barrier/<project>/lg-<...> ref), recorded before the member loop, is NOT dropped on those same 5 aborts (adversary observed file=true ref=true). Harm bounded (default legged close uses the --merge-commit group barrier anchored at the merge commit; reconcile-running-set drops the group baseline when the descriptor persisted). Fix (S): extend the #674 abort cleanup to also drop the group baseline on the same aborts; regression asserting the lg-* file+ref are gone. R2 (pre-existing crash window, low): a SIGKILL-class hard crash between recording baselines and writing the Phase-1 running-set journal (running-set.json state:opening) strands baselines with no opening marker for reconcile to find — unreachable via any refusal return, predates #674. Fix (M): reorder so the crash-recoverable journal/opening marker is durable BEFORE any baseline is recorded, OR add an orphan-baseline reconciliation sweep. Cross-edition (adaptive-node GENERATED aggregator) → four-chain. Full body on GitHub #678. |

## Rules

- A separate roadmap/research session owns discovering and adding future work to GitHub issues.
- `workflow-next` fetches GitHub issues, mirrors active implementation work here, and advances one item per cycle.
- After each workflow cycle, refresh this file from issue state.
- Move completed workflow project folders to `kaola-workflow/archive/`.
- Close linked GitHub issues only after acceptance criteria pass.
